Operation 'generatePdf' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description lacks clarity on input requirements (HTML content vs URL selection), output format details (binary vs URL), and authentication. Added specifics about PdfOptions support, authentication requirement, and clearer distinction between sync/async return values to improve AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Generate PDF from HTML or URL
data.current_description: Convert HTML content or a URL to a PDF document. Supports synchronous and asynchronous modes. In synchronous mode, the PDF binary is returned directly. In asynchronous mode, a URL to download the PDF is returned.
data.summary_suggestion: Convert HTML content or URL to PDF document
data.description_suggestion: Converts HTML content or a web page URL to a PDF document with support for both synchronous and asynchronous processing modes. In synchronous mode, returns the PDF binary directly in the response. In asynchronous mode, returns a job ID and download URL for retrieving the generated PDF. Supports custom page formats, headers, footers, and styling options via PdfOptions. Requires API key authentication.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity on input requirements (HTML content vs URL selection), output format details (binary vs URL), and authentication. Added specifics about PdfOptions support, authentication requirement, and clearer distinction between sync/async return values to improve AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'generatePdfFromUrl' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description is too brief and lacks input/output details. Added clarity on parameters (URL and PdfOptions), return format (PDF binary), authentication requirement, and guidance on when to use this endpoint versus the generic generatePdf endpoint to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Convert a web page at the specified URL to a PDF document. The page is rendered in a headless browser before conversion.
data.description_suggestion: Converts a web page at the specified URL to a PDF document by rendering it in a headless browser. Accepts a URL parameter and optional PdfOptions for customization (page format, headers, footers, styling). Returns the PDF binary directly in the response. Requires API key authentication. Use this endpoint when you have a specific URL to convert; use /v1/generate for HTML content input.
data.reasoning: Description is too brief and lacks input/output details. Added clarity on parameters (URL and PdfOptions), return format (PDF binary), authentication requirement, and guidance on when to use this endpoint versus the generic generatePdf endpoint to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'generatePdfAsync' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description lacks input parameter details (HTML content, PdfOptions) and output format specifics (202 response, job ID structure). Added clarity on what inputs are accepted, HTTP status code, how to track the job, and use cases to help AI agents understand when asynchronous processing is appropriate.

data.current_description: Submit an HTML-to-PDF conversion request for asynchronous processing. Returns a job ID that can be used to check status and retrieve the result.
data.description_suggestion: Submits an HTML-to-PDF conversion request for asynchronous processing and returns immediately with a job ID. Accepts HTML content and optional PdfOptions for customization. Returns a 202 Accepted response with a job ID and status URL. Use the job ID with the /v1/status/{jobId} endpoint to poll for completion and retrieve the download URL. Suitable for long-running conversions or batch processing. Requires API key authentication.
data.reasoning: Description lacks input parameter details (HTML content, PdfOptions) and output format specifics (202 response, job ID structure). Added clarity on what inputs are accepted, HTTP status code, how to track the job, and use cases to help AI agents understand when asynchronous processing is appropriate.

message: Operation 'getJobStatus' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks input parameter details (jobId path parameter), specific status values, and information about download URL validity. Added clarity on job status states, polling guidance, URL expiration context, and the relationship to the async generation endpoint for better AI agent workflow understanding.

data.current_description: Check the status of an asynchronous PDF generation job and retrieve the download URL when complete.
data.summary_suggestion: Check PDF generation job status and retrieve result
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status of an asynchronous PDF generation job identified by jobId. Returns job status (pending, processing, completed, or failed), progress information, and the download URL when the PDF is ready. The download URL is valid for a limited time after job completion. Requires API key authentication. Poll this endpoint periodically after submitting an async request via /v1/generate/async.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks input parameter details (jobId path parameter), specific status values, and information about download URL validity. Added clarity on job status states, polling guidance, URL expiration context, and the relationship to the async generation endpoint for better AI agent workflow understanding.
